summary: Comprehensive backend + frontend testing of LottoPredict. 21/21 backend pytest tests pass (games, draws, stats, auth register/login/logout/me, statistical + AI predictions, saved CRUD, per-user isolation, auth guards). Frontend flows pass end-to-end: landing renders, protected routes redirect to /auth, admin login works, dashboard shows stats/hot/cold/overdue/frequency chart/recent draws, game toggle works, both Statistical and AI predictions render 3 sets with balls, save persists, Saved page lists items, delete works, logout works and re-protects routes.

minor.issue: Only 10 real historical Lotto draws scraped vs ~52 expected in spec. EuroMillions correctly returns 52. Regex in lottery.py _parse_results_html likely does not match all row layouts on lottery.co.uk/lotto/results/past (main_cls 'lotto-ball-round-1' or the smallerHeading anchor variant). With only 10 draws, hot/cold/overdue statistics are degenerate (all Hot values show 30% because many numbers tied).

issues: Values render with 'd' suffix (e.g. '10d') but represent 'draws ago', not days. Suffix is misleading; should read '10 draws' or drop the suffix.
All items show 30% because only 10 draws exist for lotto (see backend minor issue). Not a UI bug but a downstream symptom.

action_items: Fix lottery.co.uk lotto scraper regex so full ~52 recent draws are captured for UK National Lottery (compare with euromillions parsing which works). This also fixes the degenerate Hot/Cold statistics on the lotto dashboard.
Change 'draws_ago' rendering suffix in StatCard from 'Nd' to 'N draws' (or 'Nx') to avoid the day/draw confusion in the Overdue card.
critical_code_review_comments: auth.py set_auth_cookies uses secure=False. Fine for local dev but should be secure=True in production (cookies are sent over HTTPS on preview). Consider env-driven flag.

lottery.py refresh_game deletes all draws and re-inserts on every startup+ensure_data call cycle only when count is 0; that's fine, but there is no periodic refresh. Add scheduler or admin-triggered refresh (POST /api/refresh/{game} exists but is not auth-protected).
POST /api/refresh/{game} has no auth guard — any anonymous user can force a full re-scrape/insert. Should require admin.
predict_ai catches all exceptions and silently falls back to statistical. Good UX, but log the exception with stacktrace to aid diagnosis (currently only logs message).
save_prediction accepts arbitrary dict payload without Pydantic validation — no schema enforcement on game/method/main_numbers ranges. Add a Pydantic model.
CORS allow_origins uses os.environ.get('FRONTEND_URL', 'http://localhost:3000'); CORS_ORIGINS env exists but is unused.
test_credentials: admin@lottopredict.app / admin123 (worked). Tests also register a fresh test_user_<uuid>@lottopredict.app / Passw0rd! per run.
seed_data_creation: Each pytest run registers 1-2 disposable users via /api/auth/register. Not cleaned up; they remain in test_database.users. Consider a teardown to remove TEST_/test_ prefixed users.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend fully tested with pytest at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py — run with: cd /app && pytest backend/tests/backend_test.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml. Real AI (Emergent LLM gpt-5.4) call verified working. Only real issue is scraper only returning 10 lotto draws — likely regex mismatch in lottery._parse_results_html for lotto's newer row markup on lottery.co.uk.
rca of the issue: Lotto draw count of 10 (vs 52 for euromillions): _parse_results_html uses row_re that requires class='smallerHeading' anchor pattern. lottery.co.uk may have restructured the lotto listing (or lotto page uses a mix of a different anchor class for the majority of older rows). Repro: curl -s https://www.lottery.co.uk/lotto/results/past | grep -c 'smallerHeading' vs the same for /euromillions/results/past. Mitigation: broaden anchor detection or use a HTML parser (BeautifulSoup) with class~=smallerHeading and iterate lotto-ball-round-N classes (round-1..round-6 rather than just round-1).
